name: S&P 500
summary: Day 67 Tuesday — S&P 500 7,200.75 (-0.41% Mon May 4 off May 1 record close 7,230.12); NASDAQ 25,067.80 (-0.19% Mon, -0.2% Tue); DOW 48,941.90 (-1.1% Tue); VIX 18.29 (+7.7% Tue); SMH $506.79 (-0.6% Tue, +29.2% 1M, +33.0% 3M); APPLE flat at $280.14 (Mon close, intraday May 4 range $271.35-$287.21 = $15.86 swing on multi-axis kinetic-restart day); URA $54.77 (-1.9% Tue, +12.0% 1M); XLE $59.39 (+0.9% Tue); Cheniere LNG $272.85 (+1.0% Tue, -3.0% 1M); Exxon $153.69 (+0.6% Tue); Lockheed $518.15 (+1.0% Tue, -16.8% 1M); Linde $493.55 (-2.8% Tue, helium-rotation thesis FAILED). **Mag 7/AI cycle thesis VALIDATED under geopolitical stress** — Apple absorbed Mon multi-axis Iran kinetic + UAE strike + SK ship damage day with intraday swing $15+ and closed FLAT at $280. KOSPI +5.1% record same day = AI-tech demand structurally separated from West Asia risk. The May 1-3 'risk-on coordination' (S&P record + Apple +3.24% + VIX collapse to 16.88) has been partially reversed by May 4 kinetic restart but the AI-capex demand layer holds. CME June cut probability ~28%; Polymarket 56.4% probability of zero 2026 cuts. VIX 22+ trigger (long-vol entry signal) at 18.29 = NOT activated — vol regime 'elevated but not regime-shifted.'
